

| COMMISSABLE — DESIGNER MEMO Change Start Date Popup Bug Fix, Layout Redesign & Collision Error Handling March 2026 |
| :---- |

| To the Designer, The "Change Start Date" popup in the Manage Revenue Schedules modal has two issues that need to be fixed. First, when a user shifts schedule dates back to an earlier period, the system throws a collision error because the proposed new dates overlap with existing schedules — but the error message is unclear, shows raw database UUIDs, and gives no per-row indication of which specific schedules are the problem. Second, the popup layout makes it difficult to understand what is happening: the current schedule date is hidden at the far right of the selection table, the proposed new dates only appear in a separate section below, and the error state does not disable the Apply Change button. The changes below are minor but will significantly improve clarity and prevent user errors. Screenshots of both the current state and the proposed redesign are included. |
| :---- |

## **1\. Current state — issues identified**

The screenshots below show the popup in two states: working normally (left) and showing a collision error (right). Both screens have problems.

**Current state — normal (no collision):**

![][image1]

**Current state — collision error:**

![][image2]

| ISSUES IN THE CURRENT POPUP Issue 1: "Schedule Date" column is labelled poorly and placed at the far right of the schedule selection table — nowhere near the schedule number it describes. Issue 2: The proposed new dates only appear in a separate "Shift preview" section at the bottom, disconnected from the schedule selection list. There is no side-by-side comparison in the selection table. Issue 3: The collision error message is one block of dense text with raw database UUIDs (e.g. 47457c9b-e236-4891...). It does not identify which schedule rows are affected and is not actionable. Issue 4: The Apply Change button remains active even when a collision error is present, allowing the user to attempt an invalid submission. Issue 5: Inconsistent spacing and layout of the New Start Date and Reason input fields. No visual grouping or required-field indicators. |
| :---- |

## **2\. Changes required**

The table below summarises every element that needs to change, comparing the current behaviour to the redesigned behaviour.

| Element | Current (problem) | Redesigned (fix) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **"Schedule Date" column label** | Labelled "Schedule Date" — placed at the far right of the schedule selection table, far away from the schedule number. Easy to miss. | Renamed to "Current Schedule Date". Moved to immediately right of the Revenue Schedule \# column so the current date is always next to the schedule it belongs to. |
| **New date column** | Does not exist in the schedule selection table. Only appears in the separate "Shift preview" section at the bottom — disconnected from the schedule list. | New column "New Schedule Date" added directly to the right of "Current Schedule Date" in the same table. Highlighted in amber to immediately show what will change, without scrolling down. |
| **Collision error message** | Displays as a raw paragraph of text below the shift preview table header, with no per-row context. UUIDs are shown but mean nothing to the user. The table rows above give no indication of which schedules are affected. | Each colliding row in the preview table shows a red-highlighted "New Schedule Date" cell and a "Collision" status badge. The error box below lists the conflicting dates in plain English. Apply Change button is disabled until resolved. |
| **Apply Change button** | Remains active even when a collision error is shown, allowing the user to attempt an invalid action. | Disabled (grayed out) whenever any collision is detected. Re-enables automatically once the date is changed to a non-conflicting value. |
| **Layout of data entry fields** | "New Start Date" and "Reason" fields are stacked vertically with no visual grouping. The preview box floats to the right with inconsistent alignment. | Two-column layout: left side has New Start Date and Reason fields clearly labeled with required indicators (\*). Right side shows the shift preview summary box. Consistent padding and spacing throughout. |
| **Shift preview section** | Appears as a separate section below the input fields, labelled simply "Shift preview". Contains columns labelled "Schedule", "Current date", "New date" — generic terms. | Labelled "Date shift preview — confirm before applying" to make clear this requires review before action. Column headers are "Revenue Schedule \#", "Current Schedule Date", "New Schedule Date", and "Status". |

## **3\. Redesigned popup — proposed layout**

### **3a. Normal state (no collision)**

When the dates do not conflict with any existing schedules, the New Schedule Date column is highlighted in amber to show the proposed changes clearly. The Apply Change button is active. The Status column shows "Ready" in green for each row.

![][image3]

### **3b. Collision error state**

When one or more proposed new dates conflict with existing schedules, the affected rows show a red-highlighted New Schedule Date cell and a red "Collision" status badge. The error box below the table identifies the specific conflicting dates in plain English (no UUIDs). The Apply Change button is disabled until the conflict is resolved.

![][image4]

## **4\. Developer implementation notes**

### **Column changes in the schedule selection table**

* Remove "Schedule Date" from the far right of the selection table.

* Add "Current Schedule Date" as the 6th column (immediately right of Revenue Schedule \#), showing the schedule's existing date.

* Add "New Schedule Date" as the 7th column, showing the computed new date based on the entered start date and delta. Highlight amber (\#FEF3C7 bg, \#92400E text) when the date is valid, red (\#FEE2E2 bg, \#DC2626 text) when a collision is detected.

* Add "Status" as the 8th column: shows 'Ready' in green (\#16A34A) or 'Collision' in red (\#DC2626).

### **Collision detection behaviour**

* Run collision detection live as the user types or changes the New Start Date field — do not wait for form submission.

* A collision occurs when a computed new date for any selected schedule matches an existing schedule date for the same opportunity product.

* When any collision is detected: disable the Apply Change button, highlight the affected row cells in red, show the error box with the specific conflicting dates in plain English.

* Error box text format: '\[new date\] conflicts with an existing schedule (\[schedule number\]) for this product.' Do NOT show database UUIDs to the user.

* When the user changes the date to resolve all collisions: re-enable Apply Change, update row highlights back to amber.

### **Input field layout**

* New Start Date and Reason fields: left column of a two-column layout.

* Shift summary preview: right column — shows selected count, baseline date, new start date, delta months, and a note explaining that all selected schedules shift by the same delta.

* Both fields should show a red asterisk (\*) required indicator.

* Reason field is a multi-line textarea, minimum height 56px.
